summary: Tight Miracle>Bertha flow, soaring Peggy-O, high‑energy Shakedown
blurb: The second set shines with a seamless Miracle → Bertha transition, an inspired Prophet/Eyes of the World pair and a powerful post‑drums Shakedown leading into Sugar Magnolia. Jerry’s guitar is on fire throughout, while Bob Weir adds strong vocal harmonies on Eyes of the World. First‑set highlights include Peggy‑O and The Music Never Stopped.
review: On December 17, 1978 the Grateful Dead took the Fox Theater in Atlanta into a late‑year high point. The first set was solid, delivering fan favorites like Jack Straw, Peggy‑O, The Music Never Stopped and a gritty Stagger Lee that kept the groove rolling. But it’s the second set that truly lifts this show: a flawless I Need A Miracle → Bertha → Good Lovin' segue gives way to a breathtaking Prophet → Eyes of the World sequence, followed by an expansive drum & Ollin Arageed jam that erupts into a post‑drums Shakedown Street. The band rides that momentum straight into Sugar Magnolia, closing the night on a jubilant high. Across all three recordings reviewers rave about Jerry’s “on fire” guitar work—especially his soaring tone on Peggy‑O and crisp phrasing in the Prophet/Eyes stretch—while Bob Weir’s vocal harmonies on Eyes of the World add extra lift. The rhythm section is tight; Mickey and Bill lock into a driving groove that fuels the Shakedown highlight, and Keith’s keyboards sit low but supportive in the mix. Recording quality varies (a clear SBD capture versus pristine audience tapes), yet each source conveys the band’s energy, making this performance a must‑listen for any Deadhead seeking late‑’70s brilliance.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All three recordings are uniformly positive, repeatedly praising the Miracle → Bertha transition, Peggy‑O, Prophet/Eyes of the World and the post‑drums Shakedown. Multiple sources highlight Jerry’s fire on guitar and the tight rhythm section, indicating strong consensus of high performance quality.

best_recording.reason: Clear soundboard capture with good overall balance, minimal technical issues, and it showcases the standout moments (Peggy‑O, Miracle → Bertha, Shakedown) most cleanly.

key_highlights: Seamless Miracle → Bertha transition that drives the second set
Stunning Peggy‑O performance with beautiful guitar tone
High‑energy post‑drums Shakedown Street leading into Sugar Magnolia
Prophet → Eyes of the World sequence praised as “on fire”

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia delivers rapid, soaring stylings on Eyes of the World and beautiful guitar tone on Peggy‑O; his playing is described as ‘on fire’ throughout especially during the Prophet/Eyes stretch.
band_performance.Bob: Weir steps forward with strong harmonic work and vocal presence on Eyes of the World, adding depth to the second‑set jams.
band_performance.Brent: Keith’s keyboards sit low in the mix but remain supportive; despite being barely audible they don’t detract from the overall clarity.
band_performance.Mickey: The drumming builds powerfully into the Shakedown Street segment, providing a driving backbone for the climax of the second set.
band_performance.Billy: Kreutzmann’s tight interplay with Mickey fuels the post‑drums energy, making the Shakedown highlight especially impactful.
